name: American Family Field
tips: atmosphere
They have live music in the stadium and a famous sausage race you can even buy merch for. The retractable roof and tailgating scene add to the vibe.
food

The best item is the Wisconsin hot dog topped with cheese curds, a slice of cheese, cheese sauce and ranch. The sushi and spicy California roll were also surprisingly good, and there is a chicken parm sandwich.
tips

If they say the roof is closed, prepare for it to feel over 120 degrees inside, so come ready for the heat. There is also a famous beer slide, though one ride for one person can cost around 500 dollars.
getting-there
It is a clean, navigable midsize ballpark and city, easy to get around compared to a big metropolis.

review.body: A clean, solid ballpark with a fun retractable roof, sausage race and tailgating scene. The Wisconsin hot dog with cheese curds was the best food item. It is a nice but not standout stadium in a mid city.

name: Busch Stadium
tips: before
Ballpark Village right outside has a great setup with bars where you can watch the game without buying a ticket, similar to the Wrigley rooftops vibe.
food
Try the Stinger donut with chocolate and vanilla, which was a standout. The Slinger dog with nacho cheese and eggs was more divisive and rated lower.
atmosphere
Cardinals fans showed up, and the game opened with a leadoff home run that got the crowd on its feet. As with most baseball, the fan ceiling is moderate.

review.body: A historic but fairly basic ballpark. Ballpark Village and the Build-A-Bear inside are nice touches, and the Stinger donut is worth getting, but the city and stadium felt average overall.

name: Great American Ball Park
tips: atmosphere
The ballpark itself is not much special, but the view with the bridge and the river behind the outfield is stunning. The center field building looks like a steamboat.
food
Get the Skyline chili cheese coneys, basically a hot dog with chili, cheese, onions and mustard. The stadium chili runs a bit soggy and runny, but it is still good.
getting-there
It is close to Kentucky, so you can walk over the bridge between the two.

review.body: A run-of-the-mill ballpark with a beautiful river-and-bridge view but little else that is unique. The Skyline chili coneys are the food highlight.

name: Wrigley Field
tips: best-seats
There is not a bad seat in the house, but the outfield bleachers are a party in their own right with beer snakes and cup snakes. Where you sit depends on when you get to the game.
food

The Shake Shack burger and fries under the center field bleachers are among the best stadium food anywhere. Doug's Dogs in the bleachers rotate weekly, and the Greek fries come in a Cubs helmet you keep if you finish it. Tavern-style pizza, not deep dish, is the local way.
atmosphere

The classic brick, ivy and the nearly 100-year-old manually operated scoreboard give it a magical, historic feel even after renovations. The bleachers are more of a social party scene than a watch-every-pitch crowd.
before
Wrigleyville has tons of pregame options. Sluggers is a great spot with batting cages, bar food and games.
tips
Make sure you check out the Small Cheval under the center field bleachers, it is one of the best things there.

review.body: Arguably the best stadium in sports. Iconic ivy, historic feel and not a bad seat in the house, with a party atmosphere in the bleachers. The Shake Shack burger and fries are some of the best stadium food we have had, and we caught a walk-off comeback here.

name: PNC Park
tips: food

The pierogies with pot roast and pickles are a Pittsburgh staple and top-five stadium food, along with the renegade dog. There is also a Belgian waffle with candied bacon and chocolate-covered pretzels.
atmosphere
Pirates fans were into it all game, and there is a fun made-up celebration where fans wave their keys for Ke'Bryan Hayes.
tips
Pirates legend Manny Sanguillen sits outside his barbecue spot at PNC Park every single game, hanging with fans. There is also a solid Kid Zone.
best-seats
Widely considered one of the most breathtaking and underrated stadiums in baseball, with great views across the river.

review.body: An underrated, breathtaking ballpark with top-five food in the pierogies and pot roast. Manny Sanguillen hanging at his barbecue spot every game is a unique touch. Fans were into it despite a rough game.

name: Citizens Bank Park
tips: before
Xfinity Live just outside is the mecca for Philly pregame, with bars, food and a celebratory zip line setup when teams win. It is about 300 feet from the Rocky statue and a short walk to the stadium.
food

Chickie's and Pete's crab fries (fries with Old Bay and cheese dip) are the signature item. Bull's BBQ burnt ends cheesesteak is made fresh, but much of the other food is pre-made and not cooked fresh.
atmosphere
Philly fans claim to be the best in sports and can approach a 10 in the playoffs, though a regular-season game is more muted. There is a kids zone with a milkshake bar.
tips
The Schwarber burger sounds and looks unremarkable and is not cooked fresh, but the flavors win you over.

review.body: A good modern ballpark with one of the best pregame setups in sports at Xfinity Live. Most of the food is pre-made rather than fresh, which holds it back, but the crab fries and Bull's BBQ stand out.

name: Truist Park
tips: before
The Battery is built right next to the park with bars and restaurants a stone's throw away, known as the best pregame party in baseball. If rain hits you can duck into one of the inside bars.
best-seats
The Chop House Club seats are for fans who want to party, with food delivery to your seat, a beer chiller, phone charger and ordering from your seat. It is one of the coolest seat setups around.
food
The four-bagger is a giant novelty sandwich more marketing than taste, but the fielder's catch po'boy with lobster tails, fried oysters and candied bacon, plus the wings, were excellent.
atmosphere

Braves Monument Garden is a Hall of Fame style museum inside the stadium, and there is a kids zone with whack-a-mole. The famous tomahawk chop can be electric in the playoffs but was quiet at a day game.
tips
There is a zip line inside the stadium, likely the only ballpark where you can zip line, and it is a lot of fun.

review.body: Known as the best party in baseball thanks to the Battery. The Chop House Club is one of the coolest seat setups around, there is a zip line and a museum inside, and the fielder's catch po'boy is excellent. Fans were quiet at a rainy day blowout.

name: Folsom Field
tips: food
The bacon-wrapped pork skewers were a standout, and the pulled pork nachos were solid. The stadium has good variety from hot dogs and pretzels to unique foods and food trucks right outside.
atmosphere
The crowd is more chill and casual than SEC or rivalry games, and it took a Hail Mary and walk-off touchdown to really get the fans into it. Fans tend to leave early to beat the lines.
best-seats
The stadium is stunning and the Boulder campus and setting are a great time.

review.body: A stunning stadium in a beautiful Boulder setting, but the fans are notably chill and casual, the lowest atmosphere we have rated. The pork skewers are great. Worth it especially while the Sanders-era novelty lasts.

name: Bryant-Denny Stadium
tips: before

Hit The Strip, a row of bars and restaurants like the Houndstooth, for the night-out scene. The campus tailgate scene on the Quad has rented white tents set up for you, which is why locals call it a cocktail party.
food
The chicken salad sandwich with ranch, bacon and cheddar is a good combo, though the food is cold and prepackaged. Eat quick if you want to catch Dixieland Delight.
atmosphere
The fans are electric from Friday night through kickoff, and the place gets as loud as anywhere when the home team scores. The light show is amazing.
best-seats
The bleacher seating is tight since they pack in as many people as possible, so manage expectations on legroom even with a great view.
tips
Stop by the Walk of Champions and the Denny Chimes on campus before the game.

review.body: It just means more here. The campus, tailgates and atmosphere are electric and the light show is stunning, capped by one of the greatest games we have seen. The stadium itself is fairly average with tight bleacher seating, and the food is cold and prepackaged.

name: Neyland Stadium
tips: atmosphere

A checkerboard night game with Rocky Top is one of the greatest atmospheres in college football. The first defensive third down was the loudest stadium moment we have ever heard across 200-plus stadiums.
food

The club-level brisket grilled cheese, New England lobster roll and Tennessee mac and cheese were some of the best stadium food we have ever had, though that is club section, not the regular concourse.
best-seats
There is not a bad seat in the house except one famous obstructed seat you have to peek around the edge of. The concourse can get clustered and crowded.
getting-there
Getting an Uber after the game is nearly impossible and took over two hours, so plan your ride home in advance if staying off campus.
tips
Tour the campus landmarks like the Rock and Ayres Hall, and the Vol Navy fans tailgate on boats on the river.

review.body: A checkerboard night game at Neyland is one of the best environments in college football, with the loudest stadium moment we have ever heard. The club-level food was among the best we have had. The concourse gets clustered and post-game Ubers are nearly impossible.

name: Hard Rock Stadium
tips: getting-there

The stadium is not on campus, so plan for the drive. From the University of Miami area it took the crew about 57 minutes each way, and getting an Uber out after the game was a nightmare. The lot for the Ubers was a 35 minute walk from the seats, so leave early or expect a long wait.
best-seats
It is a big, modern stadium and felt close to 90 percent filled for a rivalry game, but there are a lot of empty seats at kickoff. Student tickets are completely free.
food

The concourse food was a standout. The OG smash burger was one of the best stadium burgers they had ever had, and there is caviar on chicken on a bao bun that was outstanding. Skip the gyro, it looks better than it tastes.
atmosphere

Rowdier than a Dolphins game, but the student turnout is light and a lot of fans are alumni rather than students. They pump in music. Tailgates outside range from chill to lit, and there are good ones if you find them.
tips
It is the same building the Miami Dolphins play in, so it is a quality but newer stadium with less tradition. Pair it with the F1 setup and the bridge nearby if it lines up.
atmosphere

No-joke deafening and electric nonstop from both sides, close to a true 50/50 split rather than the corporate crowd you might expect. The energy in the entry line is constant chanting, and the second half reaches a genuine flow state. One of the loudest national championship environments you will find.
food

Get Thor's hammer, a novelty meat item that is usually all show, but this one came piping hot and pretty tender for stadium food and fed about 20 people around us. A rare novelty item that bangs on taste.
best-seats

This open-air stadium is a real rarity this late in the season since most title games are in cold-weather domes. The sightlines are great even from the opposite side, and being outdoors elevates the whole experience.
before
The tailgate scene outside is a fun, hyped environment with both fan bases starved for a championship. Even if you are not a big tailgater, the energy is impressive and worth getting there early for.
tips

A home national championship is incredibly rare since most big programs are in small college towns, so a title game actually held near a competing team's home market is special. Getting in and out of the area was quick.

review.body: Our highest-rated experience. Every category scored over 8.5: a piping-hot Thor's hammer novelty food, unreal gameplay that came down to the final drive, a rare open-air championship stadium and a near 50/50 split crowd that was electric and deafening all night. The uniqueness of a home-market national championship plus the on-field access made it a perfect cap to the season.

name: Yankee Stadium
tips: getting-there
The subway is the cheap way in at 2.90 each way to the Bronx. An Uber to the stadium ran 59 dollars, so the train is the move.
best-seats
Lower-level seats near the action delivered an unreal World Series atmosphere with fans on their feet for every at bat and pitch. Premium seats here ran over 1,500 dollars for two.
food

The value bucket is about 10 chicken tenders and a huge pile of fries for 25 dollars, worth it for a group. The standout is the Lobel's steak sandwich, freshly carved meat cooked perfect medium rare, highly recommended.
atmosphere

One of the best baseball atmospheres they have rated. Yankees fans were rowdy early, and when the Dodgers came back their fans were heckling right back, so everyone was into it. The fan reaction at the clinch was special.
before
There are some cool bars outside the stadium to hit before the game.

review.body: An incredible, memorable World Series night and the second highest baseball atmosphere they have scored. Yankee Stadium itself is a fairly run-of-the-mill ballpark, but the history, the crowd, and the gameplay made it a need-to-have experience. If the Yankees ever get back, go.

name: Camp Randall Stadium
tips: best-seats
It is a nice stadium, a bit better than your run-of-the-mill venue but not Sofi-level. Camp Randall has a beautiful fall setting that just feels like football.
food

The club section food was a tad underwhelming, the shrimp cocktail was good but there were no cheese curds in the club, which was a letdown. Get the brats. The regular concessions did not look like they would blow your socks off, so chase the cheese curds elsewhere.
atmosphere

One of the best atmospheres they have seen. Jump Around between the third and fourth quarter makes the place bounce and this was one of the best Jump Arounds in years. Wisconsin is a football state and the fans are engaged the whole game.
before
Madison is one of the best college towns in the country. State Street has pizza shops, ice cream, and live music right up to the capitol. The campus around Bascom Hall is stunning.
tips
Big game weekends can pair a Friday night basketball game at the Kohl Center with Saturday football, plus storming opportunities. Walk around the W crest on the field, do not step on it.

review.body: Landed as their number one college football experience and top five all time. Madison is arguably the best college town in America, the campus is gorgeous, and Jump Around is special. The only thing missing was a winning football team. Deserves a spot in the top five all time.

name: Zoeller Field at Dix Stadium
tips: getting-there
Super easy access. You can pull an Uber right to a step from the front gate and buy your tickets two minutes before kickoff. They sell paper tickets at the window starting at 5:30.
best-seats
Wide open stadium with sight lines from pretty much everywhere, short bathroom lines, and you can find your friends easily. You can walk wherever you want, even on the field before the game.
food

Cheap food and short lines. They serve chick-fil-a and even chicken broth on the sideline on a cold night. Free pizza was floating around. Skip the cold pre-wrapped waffle, the ice cream was the better call.
atmosphere

A MACtion Tuesday night game with the worst team in college football, so it is empty like a video game, but it is a unique, low-key good time. First fans through the gate got a free scarf and hat with their ticket.
tips
It is free for students, the beer is cheap, and there is an underrated bar scene afterward around Water Street. Go once for the novelty.

review.body: Not the worst experience in college football despite hosting the worst team. The free scarf and hat, field access, cheap food, short lines, and the pure novelty of a cold MACtion Tuesday night make it worth doing once, but probably not twice.

name: Kyle Field
tips: best-seats
A nice stadium that delivered an awesome split-crowd rivalry atmosphere when the Lone Star Showdown returned after 13 years.
atmosphere

The fans were honestly awesome and the rivalry game lived up to the hype. The yelling is a real tradition, they have yell leaders, not cheerleaders, and a midnight yell pep rally the night before every home game.
before
For a rivalry weekend, look for the over-the-top tailgates, including a whole cow roasted to feed hundreds of people.
tips
Campus is in the middle of nowhere College Station, so do not expect a college-town scene right outside the stadium.

review.body: The return of the Texas A&M vs Texas rivalry made for one of the best rivalry atmospheres in college football, crowned the best rivalry over Ohio State vs Michigan on this trip. Great fans and traditions. The stadium is nice but campus is isolated and the gameplay was admittedly mid.

name: Ohio Stadium
tips: best-seats
Ohio Stadium, the Shoe, is one of the most beautiful stadiums in college football. A split crowd right down the middle of the end zones is part of what makes the playoff games here pop.
atmosphere

For the Michigan rivalry game the noon kickoff hurt the atmosphere and it was quieter than expected. For the CFP home game against Tennessee it was the loudest the crowd has ever been here, even with a near 50/50 Vol takeover of the stadium. Bring red, the playoff game was a Scarlet out.
food
Collect the free towels they hand out. Standard stadium concessions otherwise.
tips
The playoff forces the home team to play the away team's hype video, which can backfire when half the building is the visiting fan base. Big noon kickoffs can sap the energy compared to a night game.
before
Campus and the stadium are both beautiful. In Columbus, the Short North has bar after bar packed on game day.
atmosphere

The Shoe gets loud and packs a huge scarlet-out crowd, and on TV it sounded like it was rocking, but from the upper deck it did not feel deafening. The lower bowl is where the sound filters down, so the best energy is closer to the field.
food
The stadium food has stayed the same for years and is pretty run-of-the-mill. Do not expect standout concession options inside the stadium itself.
before
The campus is genuinely beautiful and worth walking. See Thompson Library and the Oval, two of the prettiest spots on a top-three-largest campus in the country, on your way toward the stadium.
tips
The campus team store and bookstore are stacked with gear, including LeBron James collabs since he reps the school. Great pregame stop if you want merch before the game.

review.body: The Shoe earns its reputation. The Tennessee CFP home game was the loudest the building has ever been and took the top spot among the playoff home games at the time, while the Michigan rivalry game suffered from a flat noon-kickoff crowd and bad gameplay. A beautiful stadium that comes alive for the biggest games.

name: Notre Dame Stadium
tips: best-seats
The Cathedral of college football. It is one big classic bowl with the famous golden dome and Touchdown Jesus nearby. The seats are old-style, the place just feels legit and historic.
food
They do not sell booze inside, so know that going in. Standard stadium food otherwise.
atmosphere

The fan section felt a little weak on the opening kickoff compared to the Penn State intro, but the pregame fireworks and the setting are electric. The first-ever 12-team playoff home game atmosphere was strong.
before

Far and away the best bookstore they have seen on the tour, you can drop serious money there. The tailgate scene is unlike any other, and visiting fans, especially in a cold playoff game, are friendly.
tips
Dress warm, it can snow. South Bend gets genuine playoff cold.

review.body: The first-ever CFP home game at the Cathedral of college football. The history, the golden dome, the elite bookstore, and the tailgates make it special, and Notre Dame put on a professional playoff performance. The atmosphere was good but not quite the loudest, landing it as a strong but not chart-topping playoff experience.

name: DKR-Texas Memorial Stadium
tips: best-seats
Texas wears one of the most beautiful, clean college football looks and the field views are great. A nice stadium overall.
food
The corn dog at DKR is a solid stadium food pick, get it with mustard.
atmosphere
For the CFP first-round game the crowd was sleepy and a bit quiet pregame, partly because students were on winter break. It picked up but never reached the buzz of a traditional college town.
before
Austin is not a traditional college town, so the frat and bar buzz around the stadium is lighter than places like State College, but the city itself is great.

review.body: Texas hosting a CFP home game at DKR. The stadium and uniforms are beautiful and the city is great, but the atmosphere was a touch flat with students on winter break and Austin not being a classic college town. A blowout win kept it from climbing higher, landing just under the other playoff home games.

name: Mercedes-Benz Stadium
tips: best-seats

A gorgeous modern dome built for big events. Comfortable, far more enjoyable for comfort than an old barn, and the upper-deck capacity is the thing to watch for a neutral-site game with a cheap ticket.
food

The Taffer Grill common-folk food is genuinely good and does not break the bank, tip the cap. If you have suite access, the mac and cheese is outstanding. Stadium pricing on the concourse is cheap and good.
atmosphere

For the Peach Bowl CFP quarterfinal the crowd was sleepy and there were a lot of empty seats until a great game woke them up, then it became one of the better atmospheres once it went to double overtime.
getting-there
The College Football Hall of Fame is right next door, a great pre-game stop with plenty of merch.
tips
Neutral-site playoff games here can have very cheap tickets, get-in was 19 dollars, and a sleepy crowd, but a barn-burner game can rescue the whole atmosphere.
getting-there
Stadium traffic getting in and out is brutal, on par with Jerry World. You cannot move freely throughout the stadium, so build in extra time.
food

The 40 dollar Barbecue Box (chicken thighs, ribs, apple pie, coleslaw, beans, cornbread) is fire and they only sell about 200 of them. The cornbread was the best stadium food anyone on the crew had ever had. Also a rare chance to eat Chick-fil-A inside since the championship is on a Monday, plus Roosters ice cream that is incredibly creamy.
tips
Concession prices are very affordable here compared to most stadiums, which is a big plus for a championship event.
atmosphere

Beautiful modern arena and a great host city in Atlanta. The National Championship crowd felt like legit fans, though you need a good bit of cash to get in. It was roughly 12 to 15 degrees in Atlanta for this game, so dress warm.

review.body: A well-deserving host for the national championship in a beautiful modern stadium with surprisingly affordable concession prices and enough die-hard fans to feel real anxiety as the game came down to one possession. The final Snapback experience score was a 7.4, landing it in the one-and-done tier. Worth the trip when your team is in the big game, but the brutal in-and-out traffic holds it back.

name: Rose Bowl
tips: getting-there
Parking lots are literally on a golf course, you tailgate on the fairways, which is a unique and great setup. It was about a 40 minute drive from LA with no traffic on New Year's Day.
best-seats

One continuous bowl with no upper, lower, or 400 levels, so there is genuinely not a bad seat in the house. The seats are tight with no legroom though, so short fans fare better. A split crowd right down the middle of the end zones is part of the charm.
food
Do not miss the lumpia, a Filipino egg roll that was maybe the best stadium food they have eaten in over 100 events. Lines for food and bathrooms were short and well organized.
atmosphere

Loud for both fan bases with a great split crowd. Games are scheduled so the sun sets behind the hills between the third and fourth quarter, which is beautiful. Premium seating gets you a premium tailgate beforehand.
tips
The granddaddy of them all, played once a year on a national historic landmark. The Rose Bowl gear is elite, the merch holds value. Try to catch the New Year's parade in the morning.

review.body: The granddaddy of them all earned the top score of the entire CFP tour. The golf-course tailgates, the single-bowl design with no bad seat, the lumpia, and the third-quarter sunset over the hills make it feel like a bucket-list event. So much prestige and tradition, only just missing the Rose Bowl parade itself.

name: Moody Center
expName: KD's Return to Austin (Spurs vs. Suns)
tips: getting-there
Plan for a bottleneck leaving the building.
best-seats
The premium suite seats are comfy but have a bit of a bar in front, so you have to lean up to get a clear view of the floor.
food
Suite spread had burnt ends, brisket, beef tacos, quesadillas, steak sliders, chicken wings and egg rolls. The steak slider was a surprise standout. The wings needed sauce.
atmosphere

Brand new arena and a solid crowd for what is effectively a Spurs home game in Austin. The split skewed toward Spurs fans rather than the Texas student or KD-jersey crowd you might expect. Note the Texas and KD signage gets blacked out for the NBA broadcast.
tips
The Spurs play here a couple of nights in a row as part of the I-35 series because the San Antonio Rodeo pushes them out of town, so you can catch back-to-back games.

review.body: A fun NBA experience if you are in Austin, with a nice new arena and good suite food, but it played out like a standard Spurs home game rather than the Kevin Durant homecoming we expected. With Wembanyama ruled out for the season and the Spurs pulling away, it lacked a special moment. Final Snapback experience score was a 6.73.

name: American Airlines Center
tips: best-seats
Even up high there is not a bad view in the house. This was the most expensive regular season Mavs ticket in history, so you do not need to go all the way down to get a great vantage point.
food

The Texas cheese steak sandwich is the food item of the game, about 20 dollars and comes with chips. Good ratio of ingredients and very good bread, though the onions tend to pile up on the edge rather than disperse.
atmosphere

An electric but emotional crowd for Luka's return in a Lakers jersey. Expect constant Fire Nico chants at the free-throw line, MVP chants for Luka, a tribute video and a standing ovation. It felt almost like a funeral or memorial before turning into a celebration.
getting-there
Huge bottleneck leaving the arena after the game.

review.body: One of the single most unique sporting events you can attend, seeing a superstar return after one of the biggest trades in sports history and torch his old team for 45 points while still getting a standing ovation. The arena is one of the nicest around with no bad views. Gameplay turned into a blowout so it lost the back-and-forth, but the atmosphere and uniqueness carried it to a 7.45 final score.

name: Intuit Dome
tips: getting-there

Entry is all Face ID. Once you set up the app you can walk straight through the door without pulling out your phone, and getting in took under a minute. The same tech lets you grab merch or food and just walk out without scanning.
food

Only two food spots, Pick and Roll and Fast Break, because the whole design pushes you to watch the game. Lines are quick in and out. The 16 dollar double cheeseburger is solid, but club prices are not included and run high.
best-seats
Every single seat has a USB-C charging port. The top corner seats are steep and the worst in the house but went for about 15 dollars and are worth it. The lower bowl is essentially all club section.
atmosphere

The much-hyped Clippers Wall behind the basket (the first 13 rows are standing-only swell seats) feels a bit forced and was not as rowdy as advertised, partly due to LA traffic and low attendance. The 40,000-square-foot Halo board overhead is insane.
tips
Most teams play the Clippers and Lakers back to back on the West Coast road trip, so catch your team here. It is a cooler experience and about half the price of the Lakers.

review.body: Steve Ballmer's 2 billion dollar arena is a state-of-the-art masterpiece with Face ID entry, charging ports at every seat and a massive Halo board. The one thing money has not yet bought is the fan energy, as the Clippers Wall feels forced rather than the European-soccer atmosphere it was meant to be. A strong stadium and gameplay score offset a weak fans-and-atmosphere score for a 7.42 final, one of the higher NBA experiences.

name: Little Caesars Arena
tips: atmosphere

One of the best basketball environments you will ever be in. The crowd was so loud during the Pistons comeback that ears were ringing for days, with spontaneous Detroit Basketball chants breaking out from the stands. Their first home playoff game in over 15 years made the building electric.
food

The 313 Grill smash burger is above-average stadium food with caramelized onions, served very hot, paired with decent fries. Little Caesars is also available in-arena if you want the on-the-nose option.
stadium
Really nice arena with an open-air feel and good views. The Pistons stepped it up for the playoffs.
tips
If the Pistons are back in the playoffs and you live near Detroit, highly recommend going. Few fan bases are as passionate.

review.body: One of the best playoff games and atmospheres ever witnessed in person, a back-and-forth thriller between the Pistons and Knicks with incredible shot-making from Cade Cunningham, Jalen Brunson and Karl-Anthony Towns. Detroit lost on a controversial no-call that was later admitted to be a foul, which kept it from going even higher, but the passion of the crowd in their first home playoff game in over 15 years made it special. Final score was an 8.03, the 10th highest ever given.

name: Glass Bowl
tips: atmosphere

The crowd is small and a lot of it crams under the one shaded section of the stadium, but everyone there genuinely wants to be there. Loud and excited in the parking lots, ready for what they came to see.
before

The pregame parking-lot scene had immaculate vibes with cornhole, a car show, food trucks, and a live band. There is also a rocket attraction nearby aimed toward Bowling Green's stadium 25 miles south.
food

Do not expect much Italian cuisine at the tailgates despite the theme. It was mostly beer and standard fare. The suite level had actual Italian food: meatballs, mozzarella, bread, and a tiramisu type dessert.
best-seats
Upgrading to a VIP suite cost only about 10 dollars more than a regular ticket and got free Italian food and drink. Worth the small upgrade.
tips

You can walk right onto the field pregame with no one checking anything. Access here is wide open, which is part of what makes it unique. Bring sunscreen and water because the sun fries the open stadium for hours.

review.body: An unusual but fun day watching the Italian professional football league play their championship in Toledo. The players truly love the game and smashed low expectations, but it was a blowout snoozer and the small stadium sat mostly empty in blistering heat. Go for the novelty and the open-container, welcoming city, not for elite football.

name: Aloha Stadium
tips: before
The tailgates are a chill, family-and-community vibe rather than rowdy. The Warrior Walk where the team arrives is a highlight. Locals love to party and are extremely welcoming, so get there early.
atmosphere

The crowd starts as a packed sellout white-out and the team entrance is electric, but fans trickle out by the fourth quarter and it is a less rowdy, more laid-back game-day feel than a big SEC environment.
food
Food trucks ring the stadium with endless options and cuisines. Standouts included the Hawaiian blue cheeseburger and a katsu plate. Try a spam musubi. Tons of variety for everyone in your group.
tips
Learn a couple local words: chee-hoo is the celebration yell when the team scores, and use 'you like go' as an invite. Fans appreciate it. It is a true bucket-list, once-in-a-lifetime trip.
getting-there
It is a long haul to get here, multiple flights from the mainland, but being in beautiful Honolulu makes it worth it. The setting and paradise location are a huge part of the draw.

review.body: A bucket-list, once-in-a-lifetime college football experience in paradise. The food-truck spread is incredible, the locals take care of you better than anywhere, and the trip itself is unmatched for uniqueness. The fan atmosphere is more laid-back than a big SEC night game, and it is currently a temporary stadium, so the score lands strong but short of the top tier.

name: Kenan Stadium
tips: before

Chapel Hill is underrated and the pregame scene is great. Franklin Street has a real buzz, and the campus is beautiful with the bell tower and the well where they say drinking on the first day of class gets you straight A's.
atmosphere

It is a wine-and-cheese crowd, more khakis and quarter-zips than deafening noise. Compared to a 19th hole at a golf course. Big pregame buzz but the in-stadium sound is not overwhelming, especially in a blowout.
food

Solid pass-fail college food. The foot-long Carolina dog with chili, slaw, and onions surprisingly worked and was not too messy. There is also a Bojangles stand, though the chicken fingers ran overdone.
food
For a great pregame bite off campus, Merritt's is highly requested for its BLT on sourdough, served in small, double, or triple sizes with a lot of bacon.
tips

You can walk onto the field at UNC during business hours, one of only a few power-five schools where that is allowed. The bookstore is excellent, near Notre Dame tier, loaded with Carolina blue and argyle gear.

review.body: Bill Belichick's college debut brought a unique buzz and Chapel Hill is a great college town with an underrated Franklin Street pregame. The crowd is more wine-and-cheese than rowdy, and the game itself was a 48-14 blowout, but the campus, town feel, and novelty made it a strong experience.

name: Autzen Stadium
tips: best-seats
There is not a bad seat in the building. Every single seat gives you a great view, and even though it is a smaller stadium fitting around 50,000, the sightlines are beautiful from anywhere.
atmosphere

Autzen gets insanely loud even for an early kick against a bad opponent. The fans showed out and it was phenomenal for the first couple quarters. When the motorcycle revs at the entrance it sends chills through your whole body and kicks up turf.
food
Concessions like the Big Ten buffalo chicken slider and the hamburger were good but ran about $30 each, so not a strong value. The ice cream sandwich was fantastic.
before
Track Town Pizza is the famous spot but it leans more nostalgia these days. Eugene is a very nice college town, though it can feel empty before students return for the term.
tips
Check the schedule before booking. The school runs on quarters/trimesters so early-season games can happen before students are back on campus, which dampens the buzz around bars and tailgates.

review.body: A beautiful stadium with phenomenal fans and a great college town in Eugene. We hit it during a blowout before students were back, so it landed in the low sevens, but for a big game this place rates much higher. Worth a return trip.

name: Brooks Stadium (SC)
expName: Coastal Carolina All You Can Eat
tips: food

Every ticket now comes with all-you-can-eat concessions. The selection is big enough to satisfy you, the hot dogs are solid, popcorn is fresh and nachos are good. The main miss is no burgers or chicken tenders in the included menu.
atmosphere
The pregame intro and fireworks were electric, but a lot of fans leave at halftime to hit the bars, so the energy drops as the game goes on.
best-seats
They recently put in field suites that are pretty nice for the size of the school.
before
Conway is near Myrtle Beach so you can play a round of golf, and there is a decent going-out scene. Best in early fall when the weather is still beautiful.
tips
The teal turf is unique. Talk to students pregame and you'll get an honest read on the matchup; they were upfront that the game would be a blowout.

review.body: The free all-you-can-eat concessions are a genuinely unique draw and a fun non-power-four experience. The fans leaving at halftime and a brutal blowout drag it down, but it is still something worth getting out to if you're in the Myrtle Beach area in early fall.

name: Vaught-Hemingway Stadium
tips: before

The Grove is the headline tailgate and many call it the greatest in college football. Tents go up the morning of, you can walk into nearly any tent and they will feed you a full plate, and the southern hospitality is real. You can rent tents, big cups and speakers.
atmosphere

Inside the stadium the crowd was honestly underwhelming for a non-conference game. Everyone says come back for an SEC night game like LSU for the real loud atmosphere. Fans leave at halftime, partly because it gets very hot.
food
Stadium food was poor. The barbecue nachos came out cold and the Grove Burger had a sneaky onion and was not cooked well. The one perk is they have Chick-fil-A inside.
best-seats
Really nice stadium where it feels like there is not a bad seat in the house at around 60,000 to 65,000.
tips
Get there at 6 a.m. to claim Grove tent space on a big weekend. Oxford itself is a great college town with good bars and eats and is not far from the airport.

review.body: The Grove tailgate is phenomenal and incredibly welcoming, and Oxford is a great college town. But the in-stadium atmosphere was quieter than expected for this game and the stadium food was garbage. Come back for an SEC opponent at night and the score would be much higher.

name: Memorial Stadium (Lincoln, NE)
tips: atmosphere

An 85,000-seat stadium that packs out and stays on its feet even for a 3:30 Michigan State game. This is a fan base that cares deeply, and they are very welcoming, friendlier than the SEC crowds. Raiola coming out through the smoke gave goosebumps.
food

Get the Runza, the German-immigrant ground beef and cabbage sandwich. It's polarizing drunk-food and heavy on onions, so skip it if you don't like onions. The Corn Husker Crusher beer is easy to drink.
before

The student tailgate is in the Nebraska Union in the middle of campus, a five-minute walk to the stadium, and drinking rules are not enforced there. Downtown Lincoln, the Railyard area, has a lot of bars and restaurants.
tips
Before the game balloons are released after the first score, a unique authentic tradition they had to bring back. They run nearly every home game as a red out.

review.body: A must-see if you are a college football junkie. The fans get it, the stadium packs out and stays loud, and downtown Lincoln surprised us with a great bar and restaurant scene. One of the warmest, most welcoming fan bases we have been around.

name: LaVell Edwards Stadium
tips: atmosphere

One of the loudest stadiums we have been in, and it stays loud consistently from start to finish, not just for a few minutes. The student section is electric, and the commercial-break entertainment with lights, music and dancing keeps everyone engaged.
food
Get the Cougar Tail, an 18-inch maple (or blueberry) donut. Note this is a sober crowd, so the famous local move is energy drinks and soda. The Swig soda shops and candy are a thing here.
best-seats
Stunning backdrop with the mountains behind the stadium. The royal-blue uniforms pop and there is not a bad seat.
before

Provo surprised us, a much better town than expected. There is one tailgate, the Blue Crew, where fans actually drink; the rest of the scene is sober and more low-key. True Religion is a great breakfast spot.
tips
This is the Holy War rivalry with Utah, church versus state, and it is genuinely bloodthirsty and underrated. Go for the rivalry game for the full experience.

review.body: Took the number one spot on our scale. The fans and atmosphere reached the nines, Provo blew us away as a town, the mountain backdrop is a cheat code, and the Holy War rivalry energy was out of control. An unforgettable student-section experience.

name: Jack Trice Stadium
tips: before

Ames has one of the best tailgate scenes in the country. The lots fill for miles with RVs, and even on a three-game losing streak in 40-degree rain everyone shows up and stays. This is where the real Iowa farmers and engineers go, more than Iowa City.
atmosphere
Notably, the colder and rainier it got, the louder the student section got. They hold down and do not leave. It is not the loudest stadium ever but the fans are loyal and built for the weather.
food
Standard stadium fare. The foot-long specialty corn dog and the chili cheese nachos are solid; getting chili on the nachos feels right for Iowa. Caramel kettle corn is a good add.
tips

Walk through the Jack Trice barrier monument, the only FBS stadium named after a Black athlete, a cool tradition with great attention to detail. They sell no booze inside but allow re-entry, so you can go back to the car.
best-seats
Visit the Campanile bell tower on campus; tradition says kissing your soulmate under it at noon makes you a real student.

review.body: Easy-living Midwest college football. The stadium and crowd are not the loudest or fanciest, and our game had low stakes, but the tailgate is among the best anywhere and the fan loyalty in cold rain is impressive. Ames would be awesome for a big game.

name: Jones AT&T Stadium
tips: before

Raiderville is a massive multi-day camp-out party outside the stadium with students living in tents and playing sports for days before a big game. The week-long energy and barbecue in Lubbock are incredible, the best campus barbecue we have had (try the burnt ends).
food
In-stadium food beat expectations. Touchdown Tavern has endless options, the buffalo chicken flatbread and the brisket sandwich were both surprisingly good.
atmosphere
When the whole stadium fills it gets loud, especially next to the band and in the student section. They added club seating sections that give parts of the lower bowl a wine-and-cheese feel.
tips

If College GameDay is in town for an 11 a.m. kick, the tailgate scene can be subdued because students are already inside and have been partying all week. Pace yourself so you peak for kickoff, not before.
best-seats
Students follow the rules here, so a respectful field-storm only happens to celebrate. The Matador song before the game is a highlight.

review.body: The overall weekend experience in Lubbock with Raiderville and the barbecue was amazing and the facilities are the nicest in football. The Saturday game-day crowd for an 11 a.m. GameDay kick was underwhelming because everyone had been up for 48 hours, which dragged the in-stadium score.

name: Jordan-Hare Stadium
tips: atmosphere

Inside Jordan-Hare the crowd is rocking and it builds in levels through the game, a veteran crowd that keeps getting louder. The outside tailgate atmosphere, like most SEC, is chill and low-key rather than rowdy.
food
There was a 30-minute line for the Bell soft pretzel. It is good if you are cold and have had a few beers, but the long wait makes it tough to fully recommend.
before

Toomer's Corner and the famous oak trees are the iconic spot; fans roll them with toilet paper after a big win, one of the coolest traditions in college sports. The eagle flight before kickoff and dotting the field are elite traditions.
tips
This is the Iron Bowl rivalry with Alabama, rich in history and hatred, though the heat has cooled a touch during Auburn's down stretch. Auburn campus itself did not blow us away.

review.body: The traditions here are among the best in college football, from the eagle flight to Toomer's Corner rolling, and inside the stadium it gets genuinely loud. The outside tailgate was a little underwhelming SEC-chill, but the Iron Bowl history and a one-possession finish made it a bucket-list game.

name: Williams-Brice Stadium
tips: atmosphere

The students showed up and did their part and it got genuinely loud, but for this rivalry game with both teams struggling it was not the rowdiest the stadium gets. A sellout crowd, but a bit of a misery-loves-company vibe given the records.
before

Lots of house-divided tailgates with Clemson and Carolina fans mixed together. The hatred is real, this is the Palmetto Bowl and fans say they do not care about the record as long as they beat their rival.
tips
The 2001 Space Odyssey entrance is a signature, though it hits harder in a winning season. Sandstorm is a fun every-game tradition. Bring layers, it can be in the low 30s.
best-seats
Great campus and at night the bars around Columbia were buzzing.

review.body: Williams-Brice gets credit for a sellout and the students brought it late, but for a year where both rivals were down the energy was just fine and the entrance traditions did not pop. The campus is solid and the night bar scene is good. Not the year to catch this game.

name: Doyt L. Perry Stadium
tips: atmosphere

This is midweek MACtion, a Tuesday-night game in 30-degree cold with rain or snow. Embrace the madness; chaotic plays, multiple fumbles per drive, and a small but devoted shirtless crowd. By the fourth quarter most of the student section has cleared out, and that is fine.
food

Smaller schools tend to have the better food, and Bowling Green delivered. The stuffed cheddar-jalapeno item was good and the bucket of donuts was a happy surprise. They even have a local beer, the Juniper ale.
before
Dress for the cold; you exhaust more energy when freezing. People hyped hitting the bars after, but realistically you will just want a warm bed after sitting in the cold all night.
tips
You can roam anywhere and easily find your group by looking at the big screen during commercial breaks. Pudge the team's animal mascot is an internet sensation worth seeking out.

review.body: A true sicko game and a wonderful breath of fresh air, college football in its purest form away from the talking heads. Freezing cold Tuesday-night MACtion in the middle of Ohio is gritty, chaotic and genuinely fun. Worth doing at least one game like this a year.

name: Memorial Stadium (Norman, OK)
tips: getting-there
Old stadium that can be pretty difficult to navigate at times, so leave yourself extra time to find your way around. The fans pack into Norman in full force for a home playoff game.
before

Get to campus early to soak in the tailgate scene. They block off the streets near Lloyd's and O'Connell's about four hours before kick and the bars, frats and tailgates are all going. Grab official CFP and team merch at the stadium since that is where the official playoff gear is sold, not the bookstore.
food

Get the state fair corn dogs, they were elite. The beef dog with pulled pork, coleslaw, fried onions and a creamy cheese sauce was immaculate, and the Fletcher's jalapeno corn dog is a Texas state fair classic worth trying.
atmosphere

The stadium entrance is one of the better ones you will see, with firework flags, fireworks out of the drum line, the Sooner Schooner and the team running out. The building gets juiced and rocking for a big night game, on par with Notre Dame but a bit more man of the people.
best-seats
An old-style bowl where sound funnels and stays loud. Being close to the field gives a great feel, but expect the place to empty out fast if the game gets out of hand.

review.body: A big game in Norman is pound for pound a great college football experience. The stadium was absolutely juiced and rocking with everything a home playoff game should be: the bars, the frats, the entrance, the pregame, the fireworks and elite corn dogs. The old stadium can be tough to navigate, and the atmosphere takes a hit if the home team blows a lead, but the bottom line is a fantastic environment.

name: Albertsons Stadium
tips: atmosphere

The famous blue turf is a real sight to behold and worth seeing in person at least once, even if it is jarring at first. Crowd turnout is modest and there is not much pregame fanfare, but once the game gets going the stands fill in and both fan bases show out better than expected.
food

Do not come expecting potato dishes, the lack of potato-based food items in the stadium is the biggest disappointment. The pizza cone is a gimmick that is messy to eat, you are better off with a normal slice. Outside the stadium, Boise Fry Company is worth a stop for fries with blueberry ketchup and sweet potato fries.
best-seats

The view from up high is beautiful and stunning, and it is a very accessible, easy stadium to get around. North end zone bleachers are being converted to suites, so that area may be quieter going forward.
tips

It is a good family-friendly bowl with a fun potato mascot (Spudzy) and a little bit for everyone. Two six-and-six teams battling for a seventh win, so set your expectations accordingly, but it is far from the worst bowl game and is genuinely entertaining.

review.body: Not even close to being a punishment. Bowl games are something every big college football fan should attend once, and this one has the fun gimmick of the blue turf, a silly mascot and a good place to bring the whole family. The food was disappointing with no potato products, and it was not sold out, but once the game got going each team's turnout impressed. Recommended.

name: The Pit
tips: atmosphere

Built 37 feet underground with a low flat roof and no center-hung scoreboard, so there is nowhere for the sound to go and it bounces straight back down onto the court, making it one of the loudest arenas in college basketball. Even down 10-plus on a comeback run it got louder than some college football games. The Howl Raisers student section stands the entire game and the snake mascot is a fixture.
getting-there
Albuquerque sits at higher elevation than Denver at about 5,326 feet, then you walk down the famous ramp 37 feet below street level into the arena. The mantra here is a mile high and louder.
food

Get the Laguna Burger, which started as a gas station restaurant. Green chilies, tomato and melted American cheese on a sesame bun, cooked to order in the back rather than sitting out. The melted cheese and green chili are the secret, green chili is a must-try.
before
Basketball here has no real pregame scene, fans show up for the game, get loud and leave, so do not expect a tailgate. Check out the campus bookstore for solid merch.
best-seats
Any game and every game is loud and matters to these fans, so it is a must-go for any sports fan at least once.

review.body: The most unique stadium I have been to, built 37 feet underground with a roof that traps all the sound and bounces it back onto the court, making any game extremely loud. The crowd brought it even down double digits on a comeback. Green chili on the Laguna Burger is a best-kept secret. The only thing missing was the team completing the comeback, which would have taken the roof off.

name: Capital One Arena
tips: best-seats
Snapback sat in VIP seats practically on the floor, close enough to be right on top of the visiting team's bench. If you can swing courtside, you are within arm's reach of the players.
tips
The team store inside the arena had current player jerseys in stock, including the latest City Edition, so you can grab gear during the game.

name: Paycom Center
tips: before
Before playoff games the Thunder host Thunder in the Park right by the arena, with live music, food trucks, and ticket giveaways. It is a fun free way to warm up before heading in.
atmosphere
Loud City lives up to the name. Fans cheer through opposing runs instead of going quiet, and they keep the energy up the whole game. Locals call it the loudest building they have been in.
tips
Every seat in the building gets a rally shirt placed on it for playoff games, and there is a t-shirt exchange where you can size up to 2XL or 3XL. Wear the shirt, it is part of the deal here.
food
The chicken tinga tacos at the concession stands were genuinely good. Easy, accommodating, low-stress concourse compared to bigger-market arenas.
getting-there
Everything around the arena is easy and accommodating, from parking to getting in the door. Far less stressful than fighting crowds and ticket scalpers at bigger-market buildings.

name: Mountain America Stadium
tips: atmosphere

After a monsoon rain stopped, it was basically a sellout and loud the entire game. Our section next to the student section was the only one standing the whole game. Everyone who had a ticket showed up despite the weather.
food

One of the best regular stadium-food scores we have given with no special access. The honey chicken tenders and the birria taco specialty item were both excellent, and the cheeseburger hit the spot too.
before
Tempe is extremely nice and feels more like a college city than a college town. Old Town Scottsdale is one of the best going-out scenes in the country, and Phoenix is about 20 minutes away.
tips
The stadium is built into the desert backdrop and looks great. Beware the rare monsoon rain, locals say it happens about five times a year but when it hits it really hits.
best-seats
Sit near the student section for the loudest, most consistent standing energy in the building.

review.body: A great experience just outside the top tier. The stadium is a little old but the gameplay, the surprisingly elite concession food, and the Tempe and Scottsdale scene make it well worth a stop if you are on the West Coast. You can build a whole sports trip around it.

name: State Farm Stadium
tips: atmosphere

For this Fiesta Bowl playoff game the crowd was roughly 92 percent one fan base, so it played like a near home game with the other side barely showing buzz. Great anthem and big game feel, though being up in a suite can dampen the energy a bit.
best-seats

We watched from one of the biggest suites you will ever be in, which is comfortable but removes you from the crowd noise. The retractable design with real grass rolled in is a highlight you can literally smell.
getting-there
The stadium uses smart two-way traffic flow for foot traffic in and out, which moves the crowd efficiently. Keep to your lane and you fly through.
tips
They bring the natural grass field in and snow it, and you can smell it. A genuinely cool stadium feature for a neutral-site playoff game.

review.body: A neutral-site College Football Playoff game that delivered with a tremendous Cinderella matchup. The retractable-roof stadium with real grass rolled in is a unique touch, and the two-way traffic flow makes getting in and out easy. The crowd skewed heavily to one fan base which gave it an almost home feel, and watching from a massive suite was comfortable even if it took some energy off the atmosphere.
